events.title: Chalk Silhouettes
events.description: Create a silhouette on a colorful background!

events.title: Thing-a-ma-jigs
events.description: Using recyclables plus a dash of imagination, construct a one-of-a-kind masterpiece.

events.title: Visit the Garden!
events.description: Come get curious in the Children’s Garden with our Garden Specialist! Ask all your nature questions, help us water the plants, discovery new colorful blooms, and learn what’s alive out there. Who knows, maybe you’ll spot a new creature while you explore!

events.title: Weaving Discovery Station
events.description: It’s May-ker Month at the Children’s Museum! Drop by the Star Bar on the 2nd floor and dive into hands-on, tactile fun at our pop-up Weaving Discovery Station! Explore different weaving techniques, experiment with looms, braid and unravel, lace up geoboards, and even weave colorful designs onto metal sphere structures. What will you create? May-ker Month is

events.title: Sunflower Thai Noodles with Edible Ed!
events.description: Sessions at 10:30am & 11:30am Celebrate Asian American Native Hawaiian Pacific Islander month with a delicious treat! Taking inspiration from his Thai Peanut Noodle recipe, Edible Ed will show us how to mix up our very own sunflower Thai sauce, to enjoy over scrumptious noodles! Admission into this program is free with paid Museum admission. Space is limited

events.title: Tree Bolts
events.description: Ever think to make art out of the bolts and washers and nuts you have in your home? Now you can! Join us as we use simple hardware to create trees and other designs. What will you try and make?

events.title: Linear Creations
events.description: Make art with string! Using cardboard shapes and colorful string, participants will explore patterns, shapes, and repetition to create unique designs. This hands‑on program encourages creative exploration and playful problem‑solving.

events.title: Hula Performance with Kuliaikapono Hula Studio
events.description: Join us as we celebrate Asian American Native Hawaiian Pacific Islander Month with music & hula! Kūliaikapono Hula Studio will perform hula kahiko (traditional) and hula ʻauana (modern), sharing with you the spirit of aloha! Admission into this program is free with paid Museum admission.

events.title: Keith Haring Heart Collages
events.description: Be inspired by artist Keith Haring as you assemble your own “You Gotta Have Heart” collage!

events.title: Postcards for The Great Indoors
events.description: Stop by Star Bar on the 2nd floor and design a one-of-a-kind postcard to remember your Museum experience! Using the supplies provided, draw a picture or decorate the front with your favorite moment, then flip it over to write or draw a message to send to a friend or loved one, or keep it as a personal keepsake.

events.title: Bridges of Impact
events.description: Our city heroes have an urgent mission! Nurse Jerry needs to get from his house to the hospital to help patients in need, but the bridge is broken! Be a city hero and help build a bridge over the river so our heroes can continue caring for the community. It takes bravery to step up and help solve problems.

events.title: Weaving Discovery Station

events.title: Try-It Tuesdays: Heroic Powers
events.description: What if you could move things without even touching them? Step into this heroic training session and learn how to use the forces of magnetism to move objects with no hands at all. Behind every superpower is science!

events.title: Early Connections
events.description: Held in our cozy Threes and Younger exhibit, a program educator will be present to offer age‑appropriate provocations designed to spark curiosity and support early development. This is a relaxed, come‑as‑you‑are session. Drop in, follow your child’s lead, and enjoy a space where you can build social connections while they build early brain connections.

events.title: Blood Donation Drive
events.description: It’s Superhero Month at the Museum, and heroes come in many forms, not just those with a mask and cape. You can be a hero too! Consider donating blood at our upcoming blood drive! Reserve your appointment with the American Red Cross in advance or drop in. Walk-ins are welcome! As a thank you, all blood donors will receive a BOGO Museum admission coupon to us

events.title: Open for FREE First Friday Night
events.description: The Museum is open from 5pm-9pm free of charge, on the first Friday of each month. Space is very limited, and guests will be admitted on a first-come, first-served basis. Museum admission is not guaranteed due to extremely limited capacity. First Free Friday Nights at the Children’s Museum of Phoenix would not be possible without the generous support of:

events.title: The Accidental Crafter
events.description: Are you a reluctant or inexperienced crafter? Are you looking for new projects but don't know where to start? Join us for an hour and a half of creativity and fun as we all craft together! All materials provided. Adults only. Do you require an accommodation to participate in a Phoenix Public Library program, service, or activity? Call 602-262-4636, TTY 711,

events.title: Celebrate Our 18th Birthday with Us!
events.description: Our Birthday is June 14th, and it’s a BIG one! The Museum is turning 18, and we’re ready to party with you. Join the fun with these birthday festivities. Make Your Own Party Hat 9:00am-4:00pm Design a party hat, wear it all day long to celebrate. events.title: Members-Only Extended Play Hours
events.description: Members-Only Extended Play Hours take place on the third Friday of each month, from 4pm-7pm. Join today and begin receiving benefits like this, and so much more right away!

events.title: Father’s Day Keychains
events.description: Father’s Day is here! Visit the Star Bar on the 2nd floor and make a special gift to give to that fatherly figure in your life.

events.title: Educator Day & Resource Expo
events.description: Stop by our Educator Resource Expo, where several organizations will share information about their products and services.
